headline: IBM Brings Its Most Advanced AI-Powered Security Portfolio to Clients, and is Strengthened by Ongoing Project Glasswing Work
summary: IBM (NYSE: IBM) today announced the latest expansion of its enterprise security program for the AI era, and partners with Anthropic as a member of Project Glasswing, an industry initiative to defend the world's critical software infrastructure.

headline: Oracle IBM AI Cloud Alliance And What It Might Mean For ORCL Valuation
summary: Oracle and IBM have agreed to expand their 40 year partnership to deliver new integrated AI and hybrid cloud solutions. The companies plan to launch these joint offerings in 2026, targeting demand from enterprises using multi cloud and AI workloads. The collaboration focuses on combining Oracle cloud and database strengths with IBM's hybrid cloud and services capabilities. For investors watching NYSE:ORCL, this move comes with the stock recently trading around $186.61, with a 1 year return...

headline: Confluent Makes It Easier to Build and Secure Real-Time AI at Scale
summary: LONDON, May 19, 2026--Confluent, an IBM Company and the data streaming pioneer, today announced new capabilities in Confluent Intelligence and Confluent Cloud that streamline how real-time artificial intelligence (AI) applications are built and secured.

headline: AusNet rolls out unified customer connections platform to fast‑track services as grid demand rises
summary: AusNet, Australia's largest diversified energy network business, has collaborated with IBM Consulting (NYSE: IBM) and Salesforce to transform how customers and electricians request and manage electricity connections. The new single, unified platform gives AusNet the ability to deliver faster, more transparent services for customers and electricians while scaling to meet rising demand — a timely, future-ready transformation as Australia prepares for increasing grid pressure driven by electrificat

headline: 3 Dow Jones Stocks We Find Risky
summary: The Dow Jones (^DJI) is made up of 30 of the most established and influential companies in the market. But even blue-chip stocks can struggle - some are dealing with slowing growth, outdated business models, or increasing competition.
